title: Number of 1 Bits
problem_statement: Write a function that returns the number of ’1′ bits in the binary representation of a 32-bit unsigned integer.

examples.explanation: 11 is 1011 in binary, which has three 1 bits.

constraints: The input must be a 32-bit unsigned integer.
intuition: Use a bitwise trick that clears the lowest set bit repeatedly until the number becomes zero.

approaches.name: Brian Kernighan’s algorithm
approaches.description: Count bits by repeatedly flipping the lowest set bit with n &= (n - 1).
approaches.time: O(k) where k is the number of set bits
approaches.why_not: Checking each bit with a mask is O(32), which is okay but less elegant than the bit-clearing trick.

interview_qa.a: It removes the lowest set bit from n, reducing the count of ones by one.

interview_qa.q: Why is this efficient for sparse bitsets?
interview_qa.a: It runs in time proportional to the number of set bits, not the fixed bit width.
